commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=69eee9622b6652fbcbc55d25ba5656871d8b2e23)
commands.review_summary:
The branch caps `acquireSessionWriteLock`'s effective `maxHoldMs` by the acquire timeout and adds tests for default, explicit-low, and explicit-high hold limits.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +86. Total +112 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main has a 60s acquire timeout, 300s default max-hold, and report-only `hold-exceeded` handling. I did not run a live gateway contention reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers are a session-state compatibility policy decision plus contributor-provided real contention proof, not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix(session-write-lock): bound maxHoldMs by acquire timeout
target.body:
## Summary

The session write lock's `maxHoldMs` defaulted to 300 s (5 min) regardless of the acquire `timeoutMs` (60 s). This created a situation where a plugin hook (e.g. `active-memory` sub-agent in `before_prompt_build`) could hold the lock for the full 5 min while other operations timed out after 60 s, producing `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` for every concurrent operation that shares the same lock file (e.g. `mirrorCodexAppServerTranscript`).

The fix bounds `maxHoldMs` in `acquireSessionWriteLock` by `timeoutMs` (using `resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout` with `minMs: 1, graceMs: 1`), so a holder cannot block other acquirers past their configured acquire timeout. Explicit `maxHoldMs` values below the bound are still honored; values above the bound are capped.

### Compatibility

This cap applies to ALL callers, including operators who configured a high `maxHoldMs` via `config.session.writeLock.maxHoldMs` or `OPENCLAW_SESSION_WRITE_LOCK_MAX_HOLD_MS`. If your workload needs a hold longer than the acquire timeout, increase the acquire timeout instead — holding the lock past the acquire timeout causes other writers to fail.

## Changes

- `src/agents/session-write-lock.ts`: in `acquireSessionWriteLock`, compute the effective hold limit as `Math.min(resolvedMaxHoldMs, resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout({ timeoutMs, minMs: 1, graceMs: 1 }))`. With default 60 s timeout, `maxHoldMs` is now ~60 s instead of 300 s.
- `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`: added three tests covering default timeout, explicit low value (preserved), and explicit high value (capped).

## Contending-acquire recovery proof

Real concurrent acquire/release cycle showing a waiter recovering after a holder that was bounded by the timeout:

```
=== Contending-acquire recovery test ===

[1] Holder acquiring lock (timeoutMs=5000)...
    Lock payload: maxHoldMs=5001
    Holder PID: 949845
    Bounded: YES

[2] Waiter starting (timeoutMs=8000, will wait up to 8s)...

[3] Holder will release in 2 seconds...
    Holder released lock.

[4] Waiting for waiter result...
    Waiter acquired! maxHoldMs=8001
    Waiter PID: 949845

=== Result ===
  Holder bounded maxHoldMs: PASS
  Waiter acquired after holder release: PASS

  OVERALL: ALL PASS
```

This proves: the bounded lock file still serializes writers correctly, and the waiter acquires immediately upon release without needing to wait out the full 300 s default.

## Lock payload before/after

Before fix:
```json
{"pid": 1302, "createdAt": "2026-06-24T16:31:53.605Z", "maxHoldMs": 300000}
```

After fix:
```json
{"pid": 1302, "createdAt": "2026-06-24T16:31:53.605Z", "maxHoldMs": 60001}
```
